retry_reason: The operation is unconditional and never fails; no error path exists.
contract_summary: Ownership-swapping wrapper: atomically replaces the label pointer in orig->proxy with a new label, taking a reference on new and releasing the reference on the old label. Also marks orig with FLAG_STALE to indicate the label is no longer current. The caller must hold the labels_set write lock and must already have a valid reference on new. After the call, the caller may drop their own reference on new; the proxy now owns a reference. The old label's reference is released and must not be accessed through this proxy afterwards.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to atomically swap the proxy label, acquiring a reference on the new label and releasing the old one; the only ancillary logic is setting a stale flag.

check.reason: Only the get operation on parameter 2 (new) is valid; it directly calls aa_get_label(new) and the access path starts from the critical variable new. The put operation on parameter 1 is rejected because the critical variable for the put is tmp (the label loaded from orig->proxy->label), not orig itself. The refcount operation acts on a different object, so starting from parameter 1 is incorrect per the rule that the access path must start from the critical variable at the function interface.
